abstract.text: Sets the value of a
from a day, month, and year.
The day-month-year triplet must be valid; if you aren’t
sure it is, call
to check before you
set it.
Sets the value of a date to the date corresponding to a time
specified as a time_t. The time to date conversion is done using
the user’s current timezone.
Increments a date by some number of months.
If the day of the month is greater than 28,
this routine may change the day of the month
(because the destination month may not have
the current day in it). The date must be valid.
Returns the day of the week for a
. The date must be valid.

. If the resulting
day-month-year triplet is invalid, the date will be invalid.
-style comparison function for dates.
Both dates must be valid.
Returns the week of the year, where weeks are interpreted according
to ISO 8601.
Parses a user-inputted string
, and try to figure out what date it
represents, taking the
into account. If the string is successfully parsed, the date will be
valid after the call. Otherwise, it will be invalid. You should check
using
to see whether the parsing succeeded.
Moves a date some number of months into the past.
If the current day of the month doesn’t exist in
the destination month, the day of the month
may change. The date must be valid.

. The date must be valid.

if the date is on the first of a month.
The date must be valid.
Returns the Julian day or “serial number” of the
. The
Julian day is simply the number of days since January 1, Year 1; i.e.,
January 1, Year 1 is Julian day 1; January 2, Year 1 is Julian day 2,
etc. The date must be valid.
Calculates the week of the year during which this date falls.
Generates a printed representation of the date, in a
-specific way.

string and stores a UTF-8 result.

Monday. If the date is before the first Monday of the year, return 0.

To move forward by weeks, add weeks*7 days.

structs to a safe but invalid
state. The cleared dates will not represent an existing date, but will
not contain garbage. Useful to init a date declared on the stack.

Moves a date some number of years into the past.
If the current day doesn’t exist in the destination
year (i.e. it’s February 29 and you move to a non-leap-year)
then the day is changed to February 29. The date
must be valid.
Returns the day of the month. The date must be valid.

The date must be valid.

weeks are understood to begin on Sunday. The date must be valid.
Can return 0 if the day is before the first Sunday of the year.

year is not a leap year, the date will be changed
to February 28. The date must be valid.
